as if this region hasn't had someone to tend it in quite a while.
name: Green Fields

desc:
The land in this area is flat and unassuming, with little colour from any
source. Tall fields of hay rise three feet high and sway constantly in the
breeze. Signs that herd animals come here to feed are abundant, from small
wandering paths to many piles of dung. A tall ridge begins sloping upwards to
the north.
name: Fertile Fields

desc:
This is the corner of a bent ridge, continuing on to the north and east. The
ground remains at a constant level as the ridge travels north, but rises
slightly in the east. A sparse covering of brush can be found, thinning out to
the south. Many patches of tall and tough weeds grow between the taller bushes
and from nooks and crannies formed by rocks.
name: On a Ridge in the Plains

desc:
This ridge rises well above the surrounding plains, perhaps thirty feet in
places. The ground is a mixture of grass and stones, while an almost constant
layer of bushes grow on top of that. The slopes down in all directions are mild,
allowing mounted riders to easily access this area. The ridge continues on to
the west, becoming slightly lower.
name: On a Ridge in the Plains

desc:
The ground rises slightly here, forming a large raised area. All around you the
ground slopes slightly downhill away from this spot, creating an ideal view of
the immediate surroundings. The ground is covered with a constant green layer of
grass for miles in every direction, making the few vibrantly coloured flowers
stand out against the background.
name: Great Expanse of Plains

desc:
The ground here is rocky and hard, providing little in the way of thriving
vegetation. While sturdy grasses grow in areas with more topsoil, tough bushes
create most of the greenery. Some evidence of animals is apparent, though they
probably use this area to travel to a more fertile portion of the plains.
name: The Plains of Eregion

desc:
Patches of shrubs and bushes grow together, clustered in the more sheltered
areas. Some grass grows where the bushes do not, yet much of the ground is bare
earth and stone. Rocks litter the ground, scattered around the area. There is
some slope to the ground, creating long stretches of gentle inclines.
name: Plains of Eregion

desc:
The ground is mostly flat, with small dips and rises which have slopes too
gentle to be considered hills. The ground is covered with a short layer of
grass, much of it sheared close to the ground by grazing animals. Small piles of
stones are placed widely apart, clearing the majority of the ground for prime
grazing land.
name: The Plains of Eregion

desc:
The ground is mostly flat here, covered with a thick layer of grass. Some areas
look like they have seen grazing recently, the vegetation is unnaturally short
in those spots. Rocks and stones dot the area to the east, leading up to a
gentle incline.
name: Plains of Hollin

desc:
This ridge dominates the surrounding landscape, easily rising higher than any
other feature for miles around. At its southern tip it is almost thirty feet
high, ending abruptly in an almost vertical drop. The slope to the east and west
is less severe but will require careful navigation. The ground is bare of
vegetation in spots making footing uneven.
name: Small Rise in the Plains

desc:
There are many types of short shrubs growing here, clinging to the ridge. Many
small paths have been made in the surrounding brush as well, created by animals
forced to walk in single file. The vegetation thins out as the land progresses
southwards, giving way to grasses and tall weeds. The ridge has a steep but
constant incline to the north-east, allowing access in that direction.
name: Ridge in the Plains

desc:
There is a primarily flat landscape here, with few curves which amount to small
rises. While there is a generally healthy layer of grass, many small paths wind
through the area, creating long tracks with no vegetation. Many small bushes dot
the area, apparently faring better than the weeds, which have been eaten close
to the ground.
name: Green Plains

desc:
The land here is generally flat, covered with an uneven layer of grass. Some
portions of the area have been heavily trodden by human and animal feet, making
many small paths which wind through the plains. Grazing has shortened most of
the remaining grass close to the ground, letting as much brown earth as green
grass show. Many small stones litter the ground, dug up by the passing traffic.
name: Green Plains of Hollin

desc:
Some portions of the ground have been worn away to bare earth, due to many feet
travelling over the same path. The land is flat, stretching as far as the eye
can see in every direction. The grass here has been eaten to different lengths,
most likely sheared close to the ground and allowed to regrow before the animals
come back for more.
name: In the Grassy Plains

desc:
Short-cropped grass covers most of the ground in this flat section of the Hollin
plains. Piles of dung litter the area, left over from a domesticated herd of
some sort. Holly bushes grow in the southern area, trampled back from the more
travelled land to the north.
name: Plains of Hollin

desc:
The ground here is covered with short-cropped grass, eaten by herd animals of
some sort. There are many tracks of hoofed animals, making it difficult to
determine the path of any individual. Though worn bare in spots, this is
obviously prime grazing land for a domesticated herd.
name: In the Plains of Hollin

desc:
This appears to be the corner of a ridge, running to the south and east. The
ground is covered with a hardy layer of grass, with tough weeds growing in the
rockier areas. The top of the ridge sits over twenty feet above the surrounding
plains, but would cause no great difficulty to climb.
name: Small Rise

desc:
The landscape begins to change a little here, as you see forested land to the
west of here, and the mountains begin to flatten out a bit to the north. Despite
the change in terrain, the colour stays fairly constant, with several different
shades and hues of green everywhere, with other colours mixed in. There are even
a few birds here and there to accompany the lovely sight with their songs.
name: Forested Slope

desc:
A cheerful atmosphere brightens the journey in this lush forest, making your
footfalls light and you thoughts equally so. The trees here are not yet full
grown, as this area joins the forest with the hills to the east. As a result,
the sky is clearly visible, the trees not having aged enough to form the vibrant
canopy they exhibit to the west. A small stream flows north.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
Here the forest continues on, as lovely as ever, with the large trees spreading
their leaves above, and the large, leafy bushes and ferns that cover the ground.
There is a feeling of gaity in the air, perhaps created by the playful melodies
of the songbirds in the trees and the many animal cubs around, making mischief
and hiding from their mothers.
name: Hollin Forest

desc:
Vibrant colour strikes your eye from all angles as you walk through this
gorgeous forested area. It is easy to see why both Elf and Dwarf chose to live
in these parts so many years ago, certainly unrivaled in beauty and perfection.
You are completely surrounded by forest here, with no choice but to continue
wandering through it, to see what other splendours it has in store for you.
name: Hollin Forest

desc:
You stand in the lush greenery of Hollin Forest. Dew glistens off the leaves
above as well as ferns growing close to the ground. Birds chirp happily and
small animals are often seen scampering around. There are some odd tracks here
and there, but you can detect no feel of evil hanging anywhere in the air. To
the west, you can proceed up a small incline to a wooded ridge. From there, you
ought to be able to get a good view of the surrounding land.
name: Hollin Forest

desc:
Above Hollin Forest the ridge ascends, fairly gently, but steeply enough that
there seems to be only one path by which you might climb it. You stand high
enough that your eyes can behold all the splendours of Eregion, from the lush
hills and forest that surround you, to the imposing Misty Mountains which lie
far to the east. You may follow the tree-lined ridge to the west, or descend
into the vibrantly foliated forest.
name: A Wooded Ridge

desc:
The ridge continues here, still giving an excellent view of Nan-Eregdos and its
gorgeous landscape. Trees and bushes dot the ridge and its slopes, although not
as thickly as in the forest below, some distance to the east. It is only
possible to walk east here or try to scale the steep slope which would probably
prove a tough challenge.
name: A Wooded Ridge

desc:
It was really a bad idea to take that last step, you think to yourself after
falling to the bottom of the cliff. At first you think you must be pretty lucky,
since you seem to be unhurt after falling all the way down and landing on your
back. Then however you discover that there is no ground beneath you, and you
finally see the long, sharp rock exiting your body through your chest.
name: Some VERY Sharp Rocks

desc:
The hills here run above the once-mighty River Sirannon. They are high enough,
in fact, as to not be able to enter it from here without a short climb down to
it. To the west, the riverbed runs its course towards the sea, becoming smaller
and smaller until it is just a ribbon running into the horizon. The terrain far
to the east is dominated by Caradhras, Celebdil, and Fanuidhol, the ominous
mountains of Moria.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
The hills here are fairly grassy, perhaps not as foliated as they might have
been in days of old. Still, there is a reasonably generous amount of vegetation
to be seen around you, despite the fact that the soil seems to be not terribly
The terrain to the north of the river looks much more lush from here, even in
the shadow of the mountains of Moria.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
Overlooking the Sirannon here, is a rocky ledge jutting out from the rugged
terrain and over the river, the work of years of erosion and weathering. Those
who possess a fear of heights are very uneasy here, as it is a long way down to
the base of this cliff of sorts. Still, the view is quite nice indeed, if you
can avoid looking down behind you.
name: Rocky Outcropping

desc:
The tiny stream bends here, continuing to the east and north, winding its
sparkling ribbon through the Hollin countryside. The landscape quite lovely
indeed, although words do not seem to do it proper justice. The vibrant flora
and colourful wildflowers dotting the hills only add to the already impressive
spectacle. Even the Misty Mountains are beautiful from here, as long as you are
far away so as not to be affected by the shadow they cast on this land at times.
name: Rivulet near the Sirannon

desc:
This is nothing but the continuation of the tiny tributary that continues on to
the east and west. Little more than a trickle it is. The only sound here is the
gurgling of the water, sounding its harmony with the winds that blow softly
across all of Arda.
name: Rivulet near the Sirannon

desc:
The small stream makes a bend here to the west and south, turning away from its
ancient parent, the Sirannon, which lies further to the north. As it turns its
back on the once-great river, the tributary heads deeper into the lands once
known by elves as Eregion.
name: Rivulet near the Sirannon

desc:
Lush indeed are these hills, supported by the endless supply of water from the
large amounts of rainfall which grace this land with its kiss of life. All of
the local terrain looks much the same, in fact, and everything seems full of
floral life. Oddly enough however, there is an absolute silence in these parts,
although you can plainly see a variety of wildlife that you would expect to make
more noise.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
The downward sloping passage opens into this large cave, beneath the peaceful
hills of Hollin. The entire cavern has the smell of animal excrement, which is
in large heaps at various parts of the cave. Also scattered about are numerous
bones, mostly of fish and small animals. You can also discern, however, some
seemingly human bones. Whatever lives here would probably not appreciate any
company.
name: Subterranean Cave

desc:
Darkness prevails in this cave, the only illumination coming from whatever light
sources you've brought with you. Something rather large appears to frequent this
cave, as can be told from the tracks here. Also evidence of this are some tufts
of fur that can be seen on the walls of the cave, as if the inhabitant of this
place has been rubbing against them in an attempt to get the lice and vermin out
of its fur. The cave slopes downward, going further beneath the hill.
name: Natural Entryway

desc:
This cliff stands high above the once smoothly-flowing Sirannon. It is a good
distance down, but the cliff is wide enough as to not be at all threatening,
except for those with vertigo. It might once have been possible to dive down
into the water, if you had been an expert swimmer, but now of course that is
unwise at best, seeing as there is no longer any water to dive into.
name: Grassy Cliff

desc:
This small woodland gives you an opportunity to get off the grass for a while.
Behind the riverbed to the north and west is the Hollin Road, used for many
years by the Dwarves of Moria, and the Elves of Ost-in-Edhil. It has long since
fallen into ruin, as there is noone to undertake the repair process. Just to the
north-west is the dry riverbed of the Sirannon. It has long been the fairest
river of the Eregion, but is now filled with earth and sand.
name: Woods by the Road

desc:
The hills here stand above a tributary of the perished Sirannon, ever watching
over it while protecting it from the elements. It is possible to see for miles
in any direction here, and the surroundings are essentially unchanging - nothing
but vibrant greenery in every direction, be it grasses and fields, or forest.
The one notable exception is the impressive mountain range far to the east,
where lies the ancient Dwarven home of Khazad-dum.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
The hills here stand above a tributary of the dried Sirannon, offering a good
view of the essentially unchanging surroundings. It is possible to see for miles
in any direction, and as far as the eye can see everything is covered with
vibrant greenery, be it grasses and plains, or forest. Even the range of
mountains to the east manage to fit somehow into the picturesque scene.
name: Hilly Overlook

desc:
A good vantage point is to be found here, as can be seen immediately. The hills
here are high enough to peek over the tops of other hills nearby, while at the
same time allowing one to vigilantly watch over a tributary winding below them.
The terrain is for miles essentially the same, with large amounts of vibrant
greenery and colourful flowers, along with a forest further north. The only very
notable exception, of course, is the large mountain range far to the east.
name: Hilly Overlook

desc:
The tributary winds along through the hilly Hollinish landscape, seemingly very
carefree. In fact, almost everything here is quite cheerful indeed, what with
the mostly lush flora and variety of a animals present. Counterbalancing the
gaity, unfortunately, are the imposing mountains of Moria off to the east, which
cast their shadow on the land. In particular the hills in the distance to the
north are darkened by the darkness cast by Celebdil, Caradhras, and Fanuidhol.
name: Rivulet near the Sirannon

desc:
This is a small tributary that at one point joined with the Sirannon to the
north, adding its own flow to that of the larger stream. The rivulet itself
continues to the east, while we have hill rising on the western side.
name: Rivulet near the Sirannon

desc:
In this particular spot, the rough hills rise up to overlook the streambed of
the Sirannon on its seemingly neverending search for the sea. Your vantage point
is quite good, giving you a good estimation of the lie of the land in the
surrounding areas, which mostly consists of grassy hills, and some forested
areas as well, particularly to the north.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
Here you stand quite near the river, on almost a landing of sorts, which leads
into some rugged looking hills. Looking around at them, you can see that there
is not nearly as much grass and foliage as on some of the other hills near, for
some odd reason that you can't begin to guess. You can keep walking in these
hills or enter the streambed of the Sirannon to the north.
name: Rugged Hills

desc:
The hills here are noticeably higher than the natural landing to the west, yet
they ascend still further to the south. Throughout these hills you find small
trails here and there, evidently created by the indigenous wildlife that lives
here. Some of the tracks you find are quite small, while some are large enough
for you to fit both of your feet in together. Perhaps you should pay a bit more
attention to your surroundings.
name: Small Hills

desc:
The riverbed continues ever on to the north and west, looking as though it might
make a northward bend somewhere further east. The Sirannon now runs betwixt some
hills on either side, leaving it in a small valley of sorts, so that parts of
the banks are rather obscured in shadow. The road that faithfully follows the
streambed is still to the north.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The shallow bed of the Sirannon makes a bend here, winding to the north and to
the west. Several wilting plants and grasses struggle for survival in and around
the bed, in addition to many dead ones, having long since given up the fight to
live. They are the exception, not the rule, however, as the rest of the
landscape here is covered with vibrantly hued flora.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
Yet again the track bends to the will of the Sirannon, this time to the east and
south. Across the perished stream, off in the distance, is Dunland. It is hard
to make out any details from this distance, but it seems the Dunlandish hills
are somewhat more desolate than those of Eregion. To the north are some lush
hills, and there are signs of a forest further north. Naturally it is possible
to clamber down into the shallow bed of the Sirannon, once the smoothly-flowing
jewel of Hollin, but since dammed up, ceasing its flow.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
You trudge still upon the seemingly endless track. Once, very long ago, this
journey might have been very exciting, meeting and talking with any number of
interesting people from all walks of life. Unfortunately, you're many years too
late, and the area is now uninhabited and the track is untravelled. To the north
you can make out a way to get into some hills, and east and south is the hollin
road.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The wide trail bends here once again, winding alongside the river bed. The
landscape here is even more in the shadow of the mountains of Moria than those
to the west, even though you still appear to be a considerable distance away
from them. The lands here are still fertile however, and you wonder if perhaps
the beauty of this land once known as Nan-Eregdos is a remnant of the beauty
once fostered by both Dwarf and Elf in their alliance of ages past. You may go
east and west on the overgrown road from here.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
This was apparently a branching point of the Sirannon, back when its silvery
ribbon stretched across the land on its journey towards the sea. The bed
continues to the north, while the tributary is to the south. Also, it seems you
can venture onto a small peninsula, which looks like it leads into some of the
gorgeous hills that grace this land called Eregion.
name: Riverbed of the Sirannon

desc:
Here what had been the strong-flowing Sirannon was flowing directly to the sea,
in an area marked by the various twistings of the river. The fast flow of the
river here has once washed enough of both banks away so that to even attempt to
climb up either one could be a potentially dangerous endeavour. An adventurer of
calibre could certainly make it with no problem, however.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
The sound of the local wildlife fills your ears and you stroll lazily along this
everlasting waterway. In addition, you hear the whisper of the wind blowing
through the thick grasses that blanket the surrounding hills, particularly to
the north. The river is split in streams channels here, making the crossing
easy. It twists more to the east, bending south. From here it is possible to
continue along the meandering streambed, or to venture into some of the lovely
hills that grace this land to the south.
name: Shallow Sirannon

desc:
Winding lazily along, Sirannon bends yet again, seemingly on a whim, to the
south and east. The river is still in somewhat of a valley, yet not so much as
to totally obscure the sun, which is visible enough to highlight the vibrant
colours of the local flora, and the dew glistening off of it. A little ways off
to the north is an old overgrown road, practically the only sign that this
gorgeous place has ever been touched by civilization.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
To the east and south the bed continues on, endlessly searching for its source.
Also you can climb out of the riverbed north or west, to reach the road. It
would be best to go that way, as the hills to the south look rather ominous,
despite their lushness, and it may be a good idea to get as far from them as
possible.
name: Streambed of the Sirannon

desc:
You're still on this wide grassy track, once travelled by the natives of both
Eregion and Dunland. That was a long time ago, and now there is hardly any sign
of use remaining, in that you see no footprints, no cart tracks, only the
knowledge that long ago this was a major thoroughfare indeed. To the south you
see an old streambed, and to the north are some rugged hills.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
You warily move around another bend in this trail, stepping lightly so as not to
make a single sound. Occasionally you come across a mangled animal carcass
rotting on the ground, torn apart by something not terribly friendly. After
stopping for a moment, you press on, ready for whatever may come. This path
seems to take you between some high banks of earth, eroding and not safe to
climb.
name: Shadowed Path

desc:
Here the trail makes a smooth bend to the north and west. On the ground here and
there are fragments of what appear to be broken tiles that may have once paved
this path. It must have been a long time ago however, since the entire area is
completely overgrown now. To the east you may enter some lightly forested hills.
name: Shadowed Path

desc:
There seems to be some sort of path through these hills, judging from the way
the foliage is bent towards the ground in places. To the south lies an old
track, which once may have been well-used, but has since become overgrown with
grass. Northward, however, the hills continue as the trees thicken to become the
forest that lies even further in that direction.
name: Wooded Hills

desc:
The neverending track runs still along the equally eternal Sirannon streambed.
You feel at peace, looking at the lush flora of Hollin, which is apparently
supported by a generous amount of rain. In addition you see plenty of small
creatures playing or eating in the grass, and a number of birds soaring in the
skies above. This place would be perfect, you think to yourself, if it were not
so close to the mountains of Moria that they cast their shadow across the land.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
This is a small, yet apparently well-travelled path, running amongst some fairly
large hills. The hills are tall enough, in fact, as to totally enshroud the
trail in shadows, creating a seemingly evil shroud on the landscape. It would be
wise to be careful.
name: Shadowed Path

desc:
The trees thin out quite a bit here, as the predominantly wooded land meets an
imposing cliff face to the west. Numerous rocks seem to have fallen from the
cliff, as can be seem by simply looking around, and more rocks are falling all
the time. From here you can follow the base of the cliff up a short slope, or
head into better forested terrain to the east. To the south appears to be some
sort of path, but it is too obscured in shadow to be able to tell anything more.
name: Rocky Woodlands

desc:
This small valley abruply pinches off, leaving you in a kind of natural bowl.
The place is rather small in area, and is very nicely adorned with trees and
lush green growth. In contrast to the rocky hills around you, that do not
support such rich green foliage, the sight is definitely different. The reason
the growth is so plentiful here is apparent, as a small spring bubbles up from
the ground. Behind you the valley continues, or you may go up to the north to
walk along a wooded ridge.
name: Hills Entering a Valley

desc:
You stand now in the summit of a small cliff, excavated by the erosion in this
rocky hill. From this point you can see the landscape in every direction, hilly
and covered by the forest. To the south a dusty line marks the dry streambed of
the Sirannon. Far east you can see the white misty mountains. There is a
definite shortage of the lush vegetation that covers most of the landscape here,
what foliage there is comprised mostly of small scrub bushes. Rocks of various
sizes cover the ground in place of the plants and trees.
name: Above a Cliff

desc:
This sparse slope seems devoid of most of the vegetation that inhabits other
parts of this area. Probably due to the numerous falling rocks from the
cliffside to your west, and from the heavy shade that seems to come from it.
Loose rocks are constantly falling off the eroded hillside, and climbing up that
way seems far too hazardous. To the west you can see the familiar trees of the
forest, and what looks like a climbable ridge. In the south are some more rocky
grounds like these.
name: Sparse Slope

desc:
There is a definite shortage of the lush vegetation that covers most of the
landscape here, what foliage there is comprised mostly of small scrub bushes.
Rocks of various sizes cover the ground in place of the plants and trees,
probably coming from an old eroded cliff that rises above some distance to the
west. You can continue on these rockier grounds to the west and south, or enter
some more fertile areas to the east.
name: Rocky Ground

desc:
The gentle hills support a beautiful deciduous forest and a wide variety of
small animals can be spotted among the trees by the patient. The land becomes a
bit more rocky to the west, while to the east and south the hills continue.
name: Wooded Hills

desc:
Sounds of animal life fill your ears as you walk through these hills. Trees grow
fairly thickly here, and to the north and east the hills flatten out while the
landscape becomes even more forested. You can travel in any direction here, be
it into the forest, south into more of the hills that grace the land, or west,
into some rockier and less foliated areas.
name: Wooded Hills

desc:
The tall trees stand sentinel above, watchful yet beautiful as well, with their
many hued leaves and wildflowers adorning their trunks. Small birds and animals
are common here, paying more mind to their own business, ignoring you
completely. It is possible to continue on in the forest to the north, or to some
wooded hills which rise up to the east. To the south appears to be a small
hollow.
name: Hollin Forest

desc:
Like old friends, the hills here stand alongside a lush forest in all their
vibrant splendour for all the world to marvel at. Truly this is one of the most
gorgeous lands you have ever seen, despite the unsettling feelings that arrise
from the mountains. The beauty itself is very nearly enough to push all fear
from an adventurer heart as he reveals in this majestic work of nature.
name: Forested Hills

desc:
You stand here among these lush hills breathing in the refreshing Hollinish air,
which feels clean and pure in your lungs. From this height, the view is
breathtaking indeed, with lovely green countryside as far as the eye can see in
nearly any direction. Added to all of that is the majesty of the mighty
mountains to the east. Few things are so lovely as nature unblemished by the
works of man.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
There is a very narrow trail of sorts here, running between the thick wall of
vegetation to the north and some hills to the south. Certainly this place would
make anyone who is claustrophobic most uncomfortable indeed. The trail is as
maddeningly silent as the hills it serves to connect, which is enough to make
anybody wary, claustrophobic or not. An ancient overgrown road runs its course
to the south of here.
name: Hollow in the Hills

desc:
Colourful grasses and wildflowers surround you here, among other types of flora,
all of which is very well-watered thanks to the large amounts of rainfall this
area must get. The hills here are substantially lower than those to the south,
becoming flatter to the west.
name: Lightly Wooded Hills

desc:
The hills flatten out here, as the landscape changes to accomodate the well-
watered forest to the north. Gradually the tall grasses change to low bushes,
which grow thickly together. There are several types of animal easily seen in
these parts, from the small squirrels scampering about, to the birds resting in
the trees singing a sweet melody for all of Arda to hear.
name: Lightly Wooded Hills

desc:
Rocks dot the landscape here, managing to quell some of the smaller plants and
bushes that might once have grown here. Most of the larger ferns and trees
continue to live however, although they are perhaps not as tall or as vibrantly
green as those which grow on more fertile soil. To the west and north it is
possible to continue through these rocky grounds, while other directions
maintain the same lush flora that the land of Eregion is known for.
name: Rocky Woodlands

desc:
Utter silence prevails, making you wary as you follow this unclear trail. The
shadow of the distant mountains of Moria is cast heavily in these parts,
obscuring any light in some places, while letting it through in others, causing
shadows of the foliage to dance and flicker ominously. The Dark One's arm
reaches far indeed in these days. Hills lie an all directions, though to the
west seems to be a small trail.
name: Wooded Hills

desc:
Here the hills open up slightly, allowing an adventurous traveller to explore
them more thoroughly. The landscape is really quite lovely, with vibrant green
grasses everywhere, speckled generously with all sorts of wildflowers. Oddly,
there aren't any animals present to enjoy the spectacle, despite the fact that
there does not seem to be anything present which might disrupt their natural
habitat.
name: Hills

desc:
As this particular spot, the hills overlook the old track that runs alongside
the streambed of the Sirannon. Your position offers a view of several miles in
any direction, the lush landscape reaching the western horizon, to the imposing
mountains of Moria to the east. Perhaps this very place was used to watch these
lands in the days of old, when the friendship of the Elves and Dwarves brought
this land to its greatest glories.
name: Tall Hills

desc:
Below you, the Sirannon riverbed runs its course, having carved a path through
these hills thousands of years ago. Even if this land has increased in age, its
beauty remains untarnished by the long years. It is hard to imagine it more
beautiful than it is now, even in the days of old, when Elf and Dwarf fostered
their unique friendship, the likes of which may never be seen again.
name: Hills of Hollin

desc:
The ancient track stretches ever on alongside its old friend, the river
Sirannon. Even with the stream dried, the land seems not to suffer at all, as is
evidenced by the lush greenery and vibrant colours present in the local flora,
which consists of everything from grasses and wildflowers to a healthy looking
forest to the north. The trees are so large, in fact, as to cast somewhat of a
shadow on the hills surrounding it, causing them to look rather foreboding.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
Yet again, the grassy track bends along beside the Sirannon. The scenery here is
most picturesque indeed, and looks like something out of a faerie tale; complete
with the wild animals, the grassy hills and the ominous looking mountains to the
east. Despite the gorgeous surroundings and the air of happiness and
tranquility, some sort of shadow seems to be over this land, perhaps a remnant
of days long forgotten.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The track here winds lazily along with the will of the indominable Sirannon.
Grass covers most of the track here, but is still worn in spots, probably the
work of some animals that frequent the area. Signs of the civilizations that
once stretched across the lands are quite absent however, and you wonder how all
traces of the kingdoms of Eregion and Dunland could appear to have simply
vanished. Perhaps the land itself remembers its former inhabitants, but you see
no signs of them as you continue your journey through Hollin.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
The track bends here to the west and north, matching the curve of the Sirannon,
whose bed runs its course just to the south of road. Far north are some lush
hills that look high enough as to be somewhat difficult to get through. To the
north of those lie some forested land, although you cannot immediately tell just
how dense the forest gets. There are all sorts of animals about, playing in the
grass or searching for food.
name: Hollin Road

desc:
This cave is rather dark and wet. It was dug by the Sirannon many years ago,
when the level of the river was higher than now. The walls are made of smooth
rock, polished by the stream during the centuries, but now covered by moss. An
acid smell and the white guano on the ground prove that birds or bats choose to
live in this damp cave.
name: Damp Cave

desc:
The ground here is slightly inclined, rising as it travels to the west. Although
a layer of grass covers much of the ground, the dominant vegetation is
definitely the many types of bushes and shrubs growing everywhere. Holly bushes
grow close to patches of nettles, creating an intricate maze which can easily
leave a traveller lost and scratched. Portions of the ground under the brush is
bare earth, worn down by animals seeking shelter for the night.
name: Lightly Brushed Slope

desc:
The riverbank becomes quite shallow here, not like the areas of steep riverbank
further upstream. The flat riverbanks seem to lead to some type of road or
track, and this place must once have been a ford when the river was in full
flow. The riverbed itself continues to the north or south.
name: Dried-up Ford on the Stream

desc:
You step out of the old tavern and onto an open deck. Around you are the rotting
remains of some small tables with old chairs and stools around them. Looking
through the gaps in the floorboards you can see a small, muddy stream flowing
underneath. The only exit is back to the south.
name: A Deck
